label: SAFT & Token Warrants
description: SAFT portfolio tracker with vesting timeline, token warrant details, and treasury position overview.

0.1.note: WidgetErrorBoundary in widget-wrapper.tsx wraps all widgets globally.

0.5.note: No lib/mocks imports in widget file (context re-exports SAFTRecord type; widget imports only from context).

0.8.note: added error guard using context error field — renders "Could not load account data" in rose-400.

0.9.note: No dead code (disabled Save SAFT button is intentional placeholder per widget description "demo data").

l0.notes: 0.1 WidgetErrorBoundary in widget-wrapper.tsx wraps all widgets globally. 0.2 requiredEntitlements=["execution-basic","execution-full"]. 0.3 availableOn=["accounts"]. 0.4 No any/ts-ignore. 0.5 No lib/mocks imports in widget file (context re-exports SAFTRecord type; widget imports only from context). 0.6 Spinner shown when isLoading. 0.7 "No SAFT records" shown when saftRecords.length===0. 0.8 FIXED: added error guard using context error field — renders "Could not load account data" in rose-400. 0.9 No dead code (disabled Save SAFT button is intentional placeholder per widget description "demo data").

1.1.note: Data from useAccountsData() → saftRecords (no inline fetch or import).

1.2.note: Context calls useGlobalScope(); balances filtered by org venue membership via orgVenues memo. saftRecords are mock/demo (MOCK_SAFTS hardcoded) — no scope filter applicable for SAFT records since they represent user-level investment agreements, not trading positions. 1.3-1.5 N/A for saftRecords: SAFT agreements are user-level, not org/client/strategy scoped. Balances (used for isLoading/error state) do filter by org.

1.3.note: 1.5 N/A for saftRecords: SAFT agreements are user-level, not org/client/strategy scoped. Balances (used for isLoading/error state) do filter by org.

1.5.note: for saftRecords: SAFT agreements are user-level, not org/client/strategy scoped. Balances (used for isLoading/error state) do filter by org.

1.6.note: Empty orgVenues (scope.organizationIds=[]) → orgVenues=null → all balances returned.

1.7.note: MOCK_SAFTS lives in lib/mocks/fixtures/trading-pages.ts — context-level concern. Widget itself has no reference to lib/mocks/.

Balances (used for isLoading/error state) do filter by org. 1.6 Empty orgVenues (scope.organizationIds=[]) → orgVenues=null → all balances returned. 1.7 MOCK_SAFTS lives in lib/mocks/fixtures/trading-pages.ts — context-level concern. Widget itself has no reference to lib/mocks/.

knownIssues: Context issue (not widget): accounts-data-context.tsx sources saftRecords from MOCK_SAFTS (lib/mocks/fixtures/trading-pages.ts) with no real fetch. There is no saft-specific loading or error state in context. Widget uses the balances error field as a proxy. Proper fix: move SAFTRecord to lib/types/, add a useSaftRecords() hook backed by a real or mock API, wire dedicated loading/error fields in context.
Save SAFT button in Add SAFT dialog is permanently disabled — form has no submit handler. This is marked as demo/placeholder per widget description. Track as L4 functional gap.
findings: L0.8 FIX: added error state guard (error from useAccountsData) — renders rose-400 message. One-line change + prettier.
L1.2-1.5 N/A for saftRecords: SAFT agreements are user-level investment records; org/client/strategy scope filtering is not applicable to this data type.
Timeline constants (timelineStart/timelineEnd) are module-level hardcoded dates (2025-06-01 to 2028-09-01). These are display-range constants, not data — acceptable for current demo scope.
